title: -> tcp-ip/blocking

examples: // Information about socket server. var SERVER_INFO = { NAME : 'server', IP: '127.0.0.1', PORT: 5555 }; function BeforeStart() { socketCreate(SERVER_INFO.NAME, SERVER_INFO.IP, SERVER_INFO.PORT); // register connect callback socketAddListener(SERVER_INFO.NAME, "connection", function () { message(">>>> on connection"); }); // register close callback socketAddListener(SERVER_INFO.NAME, "close", function () { message(">>>> on close"); }); socketOpen(SERVER_INFO.NAME); socketWaitConnection(SERVER_INFO.NAME, 5000); } function ProgramStart() { // server send : (489.78, -270.5, 250.25, 180, 0, -90) // If socket don't receive data within 2 seconds, return empty array while(true) { var arr = socketReadAsciiFloat(SERVER_INFO.NAME); if(arr.length > 0) { console.log(arr); break; } wait(1000); } } function AfterStart() { socketDisconnect(SERVER_INFO.NAME); } BeforeStart(); ProgramStart(); AfterStart();

description:
Examples:
// Information about socket server. var SERVER_INFO = { NAME : 'server', IP: '127.0.0.1', PORT: 5555 }; function BeforeStart() { socketCreate(SERVER_INFO.NAME, SERVER_INFO.IP, SERVER_INFO.PORT); // register connect callback socketAddListener(SERVER_INFO.NAME, "connection", function () { message(">>>> on connection"); }); // register close callback socketAddListener(SERVER_INFO.NAME, "close", function () { message(">>>> on close"); }); socketOpen(SERVER_INFO.NAME); socketWaitConnection(SERVER_INFO.NAME, 5000); } function ProgramStart() { // server send : (489.78, -270.5, 250.25, 180, 0, -90) // If socket don't receive data within 2 seconds, return empty array while(true) { var arr = socketReadAsciiFloat(SERVER_INFO.NAME); if(arr.length > 0) { console.log(arr); break; } wait(1000); } } function AfterStart() { socketDisconnect(SERVER_INFO.NAME); } BeforeStart(); ProgramStart(); AfterStart();

title: .stringToNumberArray
content:
Class/Module: Convert

Method: .stringToNumberArray

Description: This function split a string into an float array.

Parameters:
- str (string): Source string.
- separator (string): Specifies the character to use for splitting the string.
- limit (number): An integer that specifies the number of splits, items after the split limit will not be included in the array. (1 to 30)

Returns: → {array} . Float array.

Examples:
Convert.stringToNumberArray('1, 2, 3', ',', 3) // [1, 2, 3] Convert.stringToNumberArray('1.1, 2.1, 3.1, 4.1', ',', 3) // [1.1, 2.1, 3.1] Convert.stringToNumberArray('1.1 / 2.1 / 3.1 / 4.1', '/', 3) // [1.1, 2.1, 3.1] Convert.stringToNumberArray('1, 2, 3, , 5, 6 ', ',', 5) // [1, 2, 3, NaN, 5]

params: separator (string): Specifies the character to use for splitting the string.
limit (number): An integer that specifies the number of splits, items after the split limit will not be included in the array. (1 to 30)
examples: Convert.stringToNumberArray('1, 2, 3', ',', 3) // [1, 2, 3] Convert.stringToNumberArray('1.1, 2.1, 3.1, 4.1', ',', 3) // [1.1, 2.1, 3.1] Convert.stringToNumberArray('1.1 / 2.1 / 3.1 / 4.1', '/', 3) // [1.1, 2.1, 3.1] Convert.stringToNumberArray('1, 2, 3, , 5, 6 ', ',', 5) // [1, 2, 3, NaN, 5]

description: This function split a string into an float array.

title: -> basic-syntax/flow-control/if
content:
Class/Module: -> basic-syntax/flow-control/if

Method:  -> basic-syntax/flow-control/if

Description: The following values also evaluate to false (also known as Falsy values) false undefined null 0 NaN the empty string("")

Examples:
var x = 10; if (x > 50) { //statement1 console.log("x is greater than 50"); } else if (x < 50) { //statement2 console.log("x is less than 50"); } else { //statement3 console.log("x equals 50"); } // Comparing strings // C developers have the strcmp() function for comparing strings. In JavaScript, you just use the less-than and greater-than operators var a = 'a'; var b = 'b'; if (a < b) { // true console.log(a + ' is less than ' + b); } else if (a > b) { console.log(a + ' is greater than ' + b); } else { console.log(a + ' and ' + b + ' are equal.'); }

examples: var x = 10; if (x > 50) { //statement1 console.log("x is greater than 50"); } else if (x < 50) { //statement2 console.log("x is less than 50"); } else { //statement3 console.log("x equals 50"); } // Comparing strings // C developers have the strcmp() function for comparing strings. In JavaScript, you just use the less-than and greater-than operators var a = 'a'; var b = 'b'; if (a < b) { // true console.log(a + ' is less than ' + b); } else if (a > b) { console.log(a + ' is greater than ' + b); } else { console.log(a + ' and ' + b + ' are equal.'); }

var DigitalIO = { output: { redundant: [], general: [] } } function refreshDigitalIO() { for(var i = 0; i < 8; i++) { DigitalIO.output.redundant[i] = getRedundantDigitalOutput(i); DigitalIO.output.general[i] = getGeneralDigitalOutput(i); } } console.log("----- set -----"); // change output values. setMultipleDigitalOutput([ ioDataGDO(0,1), ioDataGDO(1,1), ioDataGDO(2,1), ioDataGDO(3,1), ioDataGDO(4,1), ioDataGDO(5,1), ioDataGDO(6,1), ioDataGDO(7,1), ioDataRDO(0,1), ioDataRDO(1,1), ioDataRDO(2,1), ioDataRDO(3,1), ioDataRDO(4,1), ioDataRDO(5,1), ioDataRDO(6,1), ioDataRDO(7,1) ]); sleep(500); refreshDigitalIO(); // get all digital output io values. console.log('output: ', DigitalIO.output); // output: { redundant: [1,1,1,1,1,1,1,1], general: [1,1,1,1,1,1,1,1] } sleep(1000); setMultipleDigitalOutput([ ioDataGDO(0,0), ioDataGDO(1,0), ioDataGDO(2,0), ioDataGDO(3,0), ioDataGDO(4,0), ioDataGDO(5,0), ioDataGDO(6,0), ioDataGDO(7,0), ioDataRDO(0,0), ioDataRDO(1,0), ioDataRDO(2,0), ioDataRDO(3,0), ioDataRDO(4,0), ioDataRDO(5,0), ioDataRDO(6,0), ioDataRDO(7,0) ]); sleep(500); refreshDigitalIO(); // get all digital output io values. console.log('output: ', DigitalIO.output); // output: { redundant: [0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0], general: [0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0] }

title: -> data-types/number
content:
Class/Module: -> data-types/number

Method:  -> data-types/number

Description: 

Examples:
var myNumber = 2; while (myNumber != Infinity) { // Execute until Infinity myNumber = myNumber * myNumber; }
---
var x = "100"; var y = "10"; var z = x + y; // z will not be 110 (It will be 10010)
---
var x = 10; var y = "20"; var z = x + y; // z will be 1020 (a string)
---
var x = NaN; var y = "5"; var z = x + y; // z will be NaN5
---
var x = 999999999999999; // x will be 999999999999999 var y = 9999999999999999; // y will be 10000000000000000
---
var x = 10; var y = 20; var z = "The result is: " + x + y;
---
var x = "100"; var y = "10"; var z = x / y; // z will be 10
---
var x = "10"; var y = "20"; var z = x + y; // z will be 1020 (a string)
---
var x = 100 / "Apple"; // x will be NaN (Not a Number)
---
var x = 10; var y = 20; var z = "30"; var result = x + y + z;
---
var x = 100 / "Apple"; isNaN(x); // returns true because x is Not a Number
---
var x = NaN; var y = 5; var z = x + y; // z will be NaN
---
var x = 100 / "10"; // x will be 10
---
var x = 100; // x is a number var y = "100"; // y is a string
---
var x = 3.14; // A number with decimals var y = 3; // A number without decimals
---
var x = "10"; var y = 20; var z = x + y; // z will be 1020 (a string)
---
var x = "100"; var y = "10"; var z = x - y; // z will be 90
---
var x = 0xFF; // x will be 255
---
var x = 2 / 0; // x will be Infinity var y = -2 / 0; // y will be -Infinity
---
var x = 123e5; // 12300000 var y = 123e-5; // 0.00123
---
var x = "100"; var y = "10"; var z = x * y; // z will be 1000
---
var x = 0.2 + 0.1; // x will be 0.30000000000000004
---
var x = 10; var y = 20; var z = x + y; // z will be 30 (a number)
---
var myNumber = 128; myNumber.toString(16); // returns 80 myNumber.toString(8); // returns 200 myNumber.toString(2); // returns 10000000
---
var x = (0.2 * 10 + 0.1 * 10) / 10; // x will be 0.3

title: .abs
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .abs

Description: The Math.abs() function returns the absolute value of a number, that is 

Math.abs(x) = |x| = {x if x > 0, 0 if x = 0, -x if x < 0}

Parameters:
- x (number): A number.

Returns: → {number} . The absolute value of the given number.

Examples:
Math.abs('-1'); // 1 Math.abs(-2); // 2 Math.abs(null); // 0 Math.abs(''); // 0 Math.abs([]); // 0 Math.abs([2]); // 2 Math.abs([1,2]); // NaN Math.abs({}); // NaN Math.abs('string'); // NaN Math.abs(); // NaN

metadata.returns: → {number} . The absolute value of the given number.
examples: Math.abs('-1'); // 1 Math.abs(-2); // 2 Math.abs(null); // 0 Math.abs(''); // 0 Math.abs([]); // 0 Math.abs([2]); // 2 Math.abs([1,2]); // NaN Math.abs({}); // NaN Math.abs('string'); // NaN Math.abs(); // NaN

description:
The Math.abs() function returns the absolute value of a number, that is 

Math.abs(x) = |x| = {x if x > 0, 0 if x = 0, -x if x < 0}

title: .acos
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .acos

Description: The Math.acos() function returns the arccosine (in radians) of a number, that is 
 ∀x ∊ [-1;1], Math.acos(x) = arccos(x) = the unique y ∊ [0;π] such that cos(y) = x

Parameters:
- x (number): A number.

Returns: → {number} . The arccosine (in radians) of the given number if it's between -1 and 1; otherwise, NaN.

Examples:
Math.acos(-2); // NaN Math.acos(-1); // 3.141592653589793 Math.acos(0); // 1.5707963267948966 Math.acos(0.5); // 1.0471975511965979 Math.acos(1); // 0 Math.acos(2); // NaN

metadata.returns: → {number} . The arccosine (in radians) of the given number if it's between -1 and 1; otherwise, NaN.

description:
The Math.acos() function returns the arccosine (in radians) of a number, that is 
 ∀x ∊ [-1;1], Math.acos(x) = arccos(x) = the unique y ∊ [0;π] such that cos(y) = x

title: .acosh
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .acosh

Description: The Math.acosh() function returns the hyperbolic arc-cosine of a number, that is 
 ∀x ≥ 1, Math.acosh(x) = arcosh(x) = the unique y ≥ 0 such that cosh(y) = x

Parameters:
- x (number): A number.

Returns: → {number} . The hyperbolic arc-cosine of the given number. If the number is less than 1, NaN.

Examples:
Math.acosh(-1); // NaN Math.acosh(0); // NaN Math.acosh(0.5); // NaN Math.acosh(1); // 0 Math.acosh(2); // 1.3169578969248166

metadata.returns: → {number} . The hyperbolic arc-cosine of the given number. If the number is less than 1, NaN.
examples: Math.acosh(-1); // NaN Math.acosh(0); // NaN Math.acosh(0.5); // NaN Math.acosh(1); // 0 Math.acosh(2); // 1.3169578969248166

description:
The Math.acosh() function returns the hyperbolic arc-cosine of a number, that is 
 ∀x ≥ 1, Math.acosh(x) = arcosh(x) = the unique y ≥ 0 such that cosh(y) = x

title: .asin
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .asin

Description: The Math.asin() function returns the arcsine (in radians) of a number, that is 
 ∀x ∊ [-1;1], Math.asin(x) = arcsin(x) = the unique y ∊ [-π/2;π/2] such that sin(y) = x

Parameters:
- x (number): A number.

Returns: → {number} . The arcsine (in radians) of the given number if it's between -1 and 1; otherwise, NaN.

Examples:
Math.asin(-2); // NaN Math.asin(-1); // -1.5707963267948966 (-pi/2) Math.asin(0); // 0 Math.asin(0.5); // 0.5235987755982989 Math.asin(1); // 1.5707963267948966 (pi/2) Math.asin(2); // NaN

metadata.returns: → {number} . The arcsine (in radians) of the given number if it's between -1 and 1; otherwise, NaN.

description:
The Math.asin() function returns the arcsine (in radians) of a number, that is 
 ∀x ∊ [-1;1], Math.asin(x) = arcsin(x) = the unique y ∊ [-π/2;π/2] such that sin(y) = x

Class/Module: Math

 Math.asinh(x) = arsinh(x) = the unique y such that sinh(y) = x

title: .atan
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .atan

Description: The Math.atan() function returns the arctangent (in radians) of a number, that is 
 Math.atan(x) = arctan(x) = the unique y ∊ [-π/2;π/2] such that tan(y) = x

Parameters:
- x (number): A number.

Returns: → {number} . The arctangent (in radians) of the given number.

Examples:
Math.atan(1); // 0.7853981633974483 Math.atan(0); // 0 Math.atan(-0); // -0 Math.atan(Infinity); // 1.5707963267948966 Math.atan(-Infinity); // -1.5707963267948966 // The angle that the line [(0,0);(x,y)] forms with the x-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system Math.atan(y / x);

metadata.returns: → {number} . The arctangent (in radians) of the given number.
examples: Math.atan(1); // 0.7853981633974483 Math.atan(0); // 0 Math.atan(-0); // -0 Math.atan(Infinity); // 1.5707963267948966 Math.atan(-Infinity); // -1.5707963267948966 // The angle that the line [(0,0);(x,y)] forms with the x-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system Math.atan(y / x);

description:
The Math.atan() function returns the arctangent (in radians) of a number, that is 
 Math.atan(x) = arctan(x) = the unique y ∊ [-π/2;π/2] such that tan(y) = x

title: .atan2
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .atan2

Description: The Math.atan2() function returns the arctangent of the quotient of its arguments.

Parameters:
- y (number): First number.
- x (number): Second number.

Returns: → {number} . The arctangent of the quotient of the given arguments.

Examples:
Math.atan2(90, 15); // 1.4056476493802699 Math.atan2(15, 90); // 0.16514867741462683 Math.atan2(±0, -0); // ±PI. Math.atan2(±0, +0); // ±0. Math.atan2(±0, -x); // ±PI for x > 0. Math.atan2(±0, x); // ±0 for x > 0. Math.atan2(-y, ±0); // -PI/2 for y > 0. Math.atan2(y, ±0); // PI/2 for y > 0. Math.atan2(±y, -Infinity); // ±PI for finite y > 0. Math.atan2(±y, +Infinity); // ±0 for finite y > 0. Math.atan2(±Infinity, x); // ±PI/2 for finite x. Math.atan2(±Infinity, -Infinity); // ±3*PI/4. Math.atan2(±Infinity, +Infinity); // ±PI/4.

metadata.returns: → {number} . The arctangent of the quotient of the given arguments.
examples: Math.atan2(90, 15); // 1.4056476493802699 Math.atan2(15, 90); // 0.16514867741462683 Math.atan2(±0, -0); // ±PI. Math.atan2(±0, +0); // ±0. Math.atan2(±0, -x); // ±PI for x > 0. Math.atan2(±0, x); // ±0 for x > 0. Math.atan2(-y, ±0); // -PI/2 for y > 0. Math.atan2(y, ±0); // PI/2 for y > 0. Math.atan2(±y, -Infinity); // ±PI for finite y > 0. Math.atan2(±y, +Infinity); // ±0 for finite y > 0. Math.atan2(±Infinity, x); // ±PI/2 for finite x. Math.atan2(±Infinity, -Infinity); // ±3*PI/4. Math.atan2(±Infinity, +Infinity); // ±PI/4.

description: The Math.atan2() function returns the arctangent of the quotient of its arguments.

title: .atanh
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .atanh

Description: The Math.atanh() function returns the hyperbolic arctangent of a number, that is 
 ∀x ∊ (-1,1), Math.atanh(x) = arctanh(x) = the unique y such that tanh(y) = x

Parameters:
- x (number): A number.

Returns: → {number} . The hyperbolic arctangent of the given number.

Examples:
Math.atanh(-2); // NaN Math.atanh(-1); // -Infinity Math.atanh(0); // 0 Math.atanh(0.5); // 0.5493061443340548 Math.atanh(1); // Infinity Math.atanh(2); // NaN

title: .hypot
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .hypot

Description: The Math.hypot() function returns the square root of the sum of squares of its arguments, that is 
 Math.hypot(v1,v2,…,vn)= sqrt(v1^2 + v2^2 + … + vn^2)

Parameters:
- args (number): <repeatable>
 Numbers.

Returns: → {number} . The square root of the sum of squares of the given arguments. If at least one of the arguments cannot be converted to a number, NaN is returned.

Examples:
Math.hypot(3, 4); // 5 Math.hypot(3, 4, 5); // 7.0710678118654755 Math.hypot(); // 0 Math.hypot(NaN); // NaN Math.hypot(3, 4, 'foo'); // NaN, +'foo' => NaN Math.hypot(3, 4, '5'); // 7.0710678118654755, +'5' => 5 Math.hypot(-3); // 3, the same as Math.abs(-3)

metadata.returns: → {number} . The square root of the sum of squares of the given arguments. If at least one of the arguments cannot be converted to a number, NaN is returned.
examples: Math.hypot(3, 4); // 5 Math.hypot(3, 4, 5); // 7.0710678118654755 Math.hypot(); // 0 Math.hypot(NaN); // NaN Math.hypot(3, 4, 'foo'); // NaN, +'foo' => NaN Math.hypot(3, 4, '5'); // 7.0710678118654755, +'5' => 5 Math.hypot(-3); // 3, the same as Math.abs(-3)

description:
The Math.hypot() function returns the square root of the sum of squares of its arguments, that is 
 Math.hypot(v1,v2,…,vn)= sqrt(v1^2 + v2^2 + … + vn^2)

title: .pow
content:
Class/Module: Math

Method: .pow

Description: The Math.pow() function returns the base to the exponent power, that is, base^exponent.

Parameters:
- x (number): The base number.
- y (number): The exponent used to raise the base.

Returns: → {number} . A number representing the given base taken to the power of the given exponent.

Examples:
// simple Math.pow(7, 2); // 49 Math.pow(7, 3); // 343 Math.pow(2, 10); // 1024 // fractional exponents Math.pow(4, 0.5); // 2 (square root of 4) Math.pow(8, 1/3); // 2 (cube root of 8) Math.pow(2, 0.5); // 1.4142135623730951 (square root of 2) Math.pow(2, 1/3); // 1.2599210498948732 (cube root of 2) // signed exponents Math.pow(7, -2); // 0.02040816326530612 (1/49) Math.pow(8, -1/3); // 0.5 // signed bases Math.pow(-7, 2); // 49 (squares are positive) Math.pow(-7, 3); // -343 (cubes can be negative) Math.pow(-7, 0.5); // NaN (negative numbers don't have a real square root) // due to "even" and "odd" roots laying close to each other, // and limits in the floating number precision, // negative bases with fractional exponents always return NaN Math.pow(-7, 1/3); // NaN

params: y (number): The exponent used to raise the base.
metadata.returns: → {number} . A number representing the given base taken to the power of the given exponent.
examples: // simple Math.pow(7, 2); // 49 Math.pow(7, 3); // 343 Math.pow(2, 10); // 1024 // fractional exponents Math.pow(4, 0.5); // 2 (square root of 4) Math.pow(8, 1/3); // 2 (cube root of 8) Math.pow(2, 0.5); // 1.4142135623730951 (square root of 2) Math.pow(2, 1/3); // 1.2599210498948732 (cube root of 2) // signed exponents Math.pow(7, -2); // 0.02040816326530612 (1/49) Math.pow(8, -1/3); // 0.5 // signed bases Math.pow(-7, 2); // 49 (squares are positive) Math.pow(-7, 3); // -343 (cubes can be negative) Math.pow(-7, 0.5); // NaN (negative numbers don't have a real square root) // due to "even" and "odd" roots laying close to each other, // and limits in the floating number precision, // negative bases with fractional exponents always return NaN Math.pow(-7, 1/3); // NaN

description: The Math.pow() function returns the base to the exponent power, that is, base^exponent.

title: .sign

 If the argument is a positive number, negative number, positive zero or negative zero, the function will return 1, -1, 0 or -0 respectively. 
 Otherwise, NaN is returned.

Examples:
Math.sign(3); // 1 Math.sign(-3); // -1 Math.sign('-3'); // -1 Math.sign(0); // 0 Math.sign(-0); // -0 Math.sign(NaN); // NaN Math.sign('foo'); // NaN Math.sign(); // NaN

title: -> advanced-data-types/array methods
content:
Class/Module: -> advanced-data-types/array methods

Method:  -> advanced-data-types/array methods

Description: 

Examples:
var array1 = ['a', 'b', 'c']; var iterator1 = array1.entries(); console.log(iterator1.next().value); // expected output: Array [0, "a"] console.log(iterator1.next().value); // expected output: Array [1, "b"]
---
var array1 = [1, 2, 3]; console.log(array1.includes(2)); // expected output: true var pets = ['cat', 'dog', 'bat']; console.log(pets.includes('cat')); // expected output: true console.log(pets.includes('at')); // expected output: false
---
-
---
var array1 = [1, 2, 'a', '1a']; console.log(array1.toString()); // expected output: "1,2,a,1a"
---
var array1 = [1, 2, 3]; console.log(array1.unshift(4, 5)); // expected output: 5 console.log(array1); // expected output: Array [4, 5, 1, 2, 3]
---
var array1 = ['a', 'b', 'c']; var array2 = ['d', 'e', 'f']; console.log(array1.concat(array2)); // expected output: Array ["a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"]
---
var array1 = [1, 2, 3, 4]; // fill with 0 from position 2 until position 4 console.log(array1.fill(0, 2, 4)); // expected output: [1, 2, 0, 0] // fill with 5 from position 1 console.log(array1.fill(5, 1)); // expected output: [1, 5, 5, 5] console.log(array1.fill(6)); // expected output: [6, 6, 6, 6]
---
var animals = ['pigs', 'goats', 'sheep']; console.log(animals.push('cows')); // expected output: 4 console.log(animals); // expected output: Array ["pigs", "goats", "sheep", "cows"] animals.push('chickens'); console.log(animals); // expected output: Array ["pigs", "goats", "sheep", "cows", "chickens"]
---
var plants = ['broccoli', 'cauliflower', 'cabbage', 'kale', 'tomato']; console.log(plants.pop()); // expected output: "tomato" console.log(plants); // expected output: Array ["broccoli", "cauliflower", "cabbage", "kale"] plants.pop(); console.log(plants); // expected output: Array ["broccoli", "cauliflower", "cabbage"]
---
var array1 = [1, 2, 3]; var firstElement = array1.shift(); console.log(array1); // expected output: Array [2, 3] console.log(firstElement); // expected output: 1
---
var array1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; // place at position 0 the element between position 3 and 4 console.log(array1.copyWithin(0, 3, 4)); // expected output: Array [4, 2, 3, 4, 5] // place at position 1 the elements after position 3 console.log(array1.copyWithin(1, 3)); // expected output: Array [4, 4, 5, 4, 5]
---
var beasts = ['ant', 'bison', 'camel', 'duck', 'bison']; console.log(beasts.indexOf('bison')); // expected output: 1 // start from index 2 console.log(beasts.indexOf('bison', 2)); // expected output: 4 console.log(beasts.indexOf('giraffe')); // expected output: -1
---
var animals = ['ant', 'bison', 'camel', 'duck', 'elephant']; console.log(animals.slice(2)); // expected output: Array ["camel", "duck", "elephant"] console.log(animals.slice(2, 4)); // expected output: Array ["camel", "duck"] console.log(animals.slice(1, 5)); // expected output: Array ["bison", "camel", "duck", "elephant"]
---
var months = ['March', 'Jan', 'Feb', 'Dec']; months.sort(); console.log(months); // expected output: Array ["Dec", "Feb", "Jan", "March"] var array1 = [1, 30, 4, 21]; array1.sort(); console.log(array1); // expected output: Array [1, 21, 30, 4]
---
var months = ['Jan', 'March', 'April', 'June']; months.splice(1, 0, 'Feb'); // inserts at 1st index position console.log(months); // expected output: Array ['Jan', 'Feb', 'March', 'April', 'June'] months.splice(4, 1, 'May'); // replaces 1 element at 4th index console.log(months); // expected output: Array ['Jan', 'Feb', 'March', 'April', 'May']
---
var elements = ['Fire', 'Wind', 'Rain']; console.log(elements.join()); // expected output: Fire,Wind,Rain console.log(elements.join('')); // expected output: FireWindRain console.log(elements.join('-')); // expected output: Fire-Wind-Rain
---
var array1 = ['one', 'two', 'three']; var reversed = array1.reverse(); console.log(array1); // expected output: Array ['three', 'two', 'one'] console.log(reversed); // expected output: Array ['three', 'two', 'one']
---
var animals = ['Dodo', 'Tiger', 'Penguin', 'Dodo']; console.log(animals.lastIndexOf('Dodo')); // expected output: 3 console.log(animals.lastIndexOf('Tiger')); // expected output: 1

title: -> mqtt/publish
content:
Class/Module: -> mqtt/publish

Method:  -> mqtt/publish

Description: 

Examples:
var BROKER_INFO = { NAME : 'broker', IP: '127.0.0.1', PORT: 1883 }; var TOPICS = { TEST : 'rodi/test/publish' } var QOS = { AT_MOST_ONCE : 0, AT_LEAST_ONCE : 1, EXACTLY_ONCE : 2 } function BeforeStart() { mqttInitialize(BROKER_INFO.NAME, BROKER_INFO.IP, BROKER_INFO.PORT); mqttConnect(BROKER_INFO.NAME); } function ProgramStart() { var isSuccess = mqttPublish(BROKER_INFO.NAME, TOPICS.TEST, JSON.stringify({ a : 10, b : "abc"}), QOS.AT_MOST_ONCE); if(isSuccess === true ) { console.log("Data transfer was successful."); } else { console.log("Data transfer was failed."); } } function AfterStart() { mqttClose(BROKER_INFO.NAME); } BeforeStart(); ProgramStart(); AfterStart();
